---
title: "MXC Format"
description: "MusicXML Compact — a 12x compression for VLM training"
---

## The Problem

MusicXML is extremely verbose. A typical lieder page produces ~14K tokens — far
beyond the 4,096-token VLM context window. Only ~10% of pages fit, and models
spend all inference tokens on XML preamble before reaching any `<note>` elements.

## MXC Design

MXC is a line-based encoding where one note = one line:

```
header work-title="Just for Today"
header composer="Jane Bingham Abbott"
P1 Voice Ob
P2 Piano Pno
---
P1
M 1 div=10080 key=-1 time=3/4 clef=G2
dir @above [font-weight=bold] Andante
N C4 q 10080 su L1:s:Lord,
N Bb3 e 5040 su bm=begin L1:s:for
N C4 e 5040 su bm=end L1:b:to
```

## Compression

Tested on 500 lieder pages:

| Metric | Cleaned XML | MXC |
|--------|-------------|-----|
| Median chars | 50,272 | 4,172 |
| Median ~tokens | ~14,363 | **~1,192** |
| **Compression** | — | **12x** |

## Round-trip accuracy

- **99.2%** lossless round-trip on 500 samples
- 3 failures from malformed XML in source data
- 1 failure from double-space in lyric text

## What MXC preserves

- Pitches, durations, types, accidentals, dots
- Stems, beams, ties, slurs
- Lyrics (bilingual, syllabic encoding)
- Key/time signatures, clefs
- Dynamics, tempo text, directions
- Composer, lyricist, title
- Barlines, repeats, endings

## Implementation

- **Encoder/decoder**: `vlm_omr_sft/mxc.py`
- **Format spec**: `vlm_omr_sft/MXC.md`
- **Unit tests**: `vlm_omr_sft/test_mxc.py` (28 tests)
- **Real-data tests**: `vlm_omr_sft/test_mxc_realdata.py` (500 samples)
